summary_one_line: Input must declare a DEFAULT namespace (xmlns with no prefix, cur->prefix==NULL) so serialization writes "xmlns" not "xmlns:".

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An XML element bearing a namespace declaration whose prefix is NULL (a default namespace, e.g. xmlns="..."), rather than a prefixed namespace (xmlns:foo="..."). The serializer reaches line 603 and the cur->prefix!=NULL test is FALSE, so it writes the 5-byte "xmlns" at line 607 instead of "xmlns:"+prefix at 604-605.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile / value_profile_cmplog winners resolve the false side (only [W] hits line 607 'xmlns'); their CMP_MAP gradient feedback rewards inputs that drive the parser progressively closer to constructing a namespace node with prefix==NULL, so they synthesize a well-formed default-namespace declaration that round-trips into the dump path with cur->prefix NULL.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive and cmplog losers only ever take the true side (line 603 BLOCKER shows W F=43 vs L T=11, and [L] marks lines 604-605 'xmlns:'); they reach xmlNsDumpOutput only with prefixed namespaces, so cur->prefix is non-NULL and the false branch is never executed (loser hitcount on branch = 0/1).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: CMP_MAP gradient: value_profile's per-CMP Hamming/prefix-distance buckets give partial credit on the comparison sequence that the parser must satisfy to construct a default-namespace node, letting it ratchet toward the prefix==NULL structure that naive (no gradient) and cmplog (gradient absent) cannot incrementally discover.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Same program-side condition (a default namespace declaration so cur->prefix==NULL at line 603), but reached by structural recombination: a generalized XML token/gap structure spliced to place a bare xmlns (no prefix) on an element.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: grimoire resolves the false side in 10/10 trials with a very high branch hitcount (625), consistent with its GeneralizationStage + Grimoire structural mutators repeatedly splicing generalized markup tokens into many valid default-namespace declarations that all flow into xmlNsDumpOutput with prefix==NULL.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog (the single-technique baseline for this pair, sharing I2S but lacking generalization + Grimoire mutators) stays blocked 8/10 with branch hitcount 0; its byte-level havoc+I2S mutations do not assemble the structural default-namespace element needed to take the false branch.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: grimoire structural: the GeneralizationStage replaces concrete byte runs with <GAP> placeholders and the Grimoire mutators splice/recurse on these generalized token structures, building syntactically valid XML that carries a prefix-less (default) namespace declaration, which byte-level cmplog mutation does not reach.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked side is the false branch of the cur->prefix!=NULL test, i.e. the default-namespace path.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
Source line: 	if (cur->prefix != NULL) {
Globally blocked side: F  (false branch)

evidence_trail.claim: The false branch writes the 5-byte string xmlns (default namespace), the true branch writes xmlns: plus the prefix.
Only the winner-resolving role hits line 607 (the xmlns default-namespace write); the loser role hits the prefixed lines.
At the blocker line the winner role takes the false side while the loser role only takes the true side.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 603  T=0 F=43  T=11 F=0  if (cur->prefix != NULL) {  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile beats naive and value_profile_cmplog beats cmplog, both on the value_profile technique delta.
value_profile's distance buckets give partial credit measuring how close two CMP operands were to matching.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
each CMP callback writes into a `CMP_MAP` keyed by (PC, operand-distance
bucket). The distance bucket is a coarse encoding of how close the two
operands were

evidence_trail.claim: grimoire wins over cmplog on the grimoire_structural delta and produces a very high branch hitcount.
grimoire resolves the branch in 10/10 trials with hitcount 625 while cmplog stays at 0.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=625  loser=0

evidence_trail.claim: grimoire's GeneralizationStage and Grimoire mutators splice generalized token/gap structures (string-based structural mutation).
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
splice and recurse on these generalised token/gap structures
(string-based, grammar-free structural mutation)

evidence_trail.claim: The single-technique delta for the grimoire pair is grimoire vs cmplog (both have I2S; grimoire adds generalization + Grimoire mutators).
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
The single-technique delta is therefore
`grimoire` vs `cmplog` (both have I2S; grimoire adds generalization +
Grimoire mutators), not vs naive.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Neither decisive technique is I2SRandReplace (Pairs 1/3 isolate the value_profile CMP_MAP gradient; Pair 2 isolates grimoire_structural), so the mandatory I2S lineage check does not apply. I primary-claim the CMP_MAP gradient for the value_profile pairs. The displayed winner-resolving seeds were stored by grimoire (some tagged I2SRandReplace in mutation_op), which is auxiliary provenance under seed_bisect's lex-min storing-fuzzer note, not the value_profile winner's chain; I did not run lineage because the value_profile mechanism is not I2S and dash rows are non-exclusive against grimoire.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where the only namespace form reachable is a default (prefix-less) namespace, yet naive/cmplog still fail to resolve the false branch while value_profile/grimoire do — showing the divergence is not driven by the prefix==NULL structural feature but by some unrelated property.

weakest_evidence_point: The exact CMP sequence whose Hamming/prefix-distance gradient value_profile exploits to construct a prefix==NULL namespace is inferred from the source path, not directly observed in a value_profile winner seed's lineage.
